mnemonics: RIP-CAL: Return, Investment, Profitability, Cashflow, Assets, Leverage (Categorizes the main types of financial metrics)
keyPoints: Financial metrics quantify a company's performance, health, and value.
R can automate the calculation of these metrics using financial data frames.
Understanding the formula and its components is essential before coding it in R.
commonMistakes: Using incorrect period data (e.g., using annual revenue with quarterly profit).
Forgetting to handle NA or missing values in the data, leading to incorrect calculations.
Misinterpreting what a metric measures (e.g., confusing profitability with liquidity).
